questions.q: By which honorific is Subhas Chandra Bose popularly known?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose is widely known by the honorific Netaji, meaning respected leader.

questions.q: Which army did Subhas Chandra Bose organise and lead abroad?
questions.explanation: Bose revitalised and led the Indian National Army (Azad Hind Fauj) during the Second World War.

questions.q: Complete Bose's famous call: 'Give me blood, and I shall give you ___.'
questions.explanation: Bose's stirring slogan was 'Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom.'

questions.q: Bose served twice as president of which organisation?
questions.explanation: Bose was elected president of the Indian National Congress twice, in 1938 and 1939.

questions.q: Bose favoured achieving independence through which kind of approach?
questions.explanation: Bose championed complete independence through more assertive and, ultimately, armed means.

questions.q: What is the English meaning of 'Azad Hind Fauj', the name linked with Bose?
questions.explanation: Azad Hind Fauj translates to the Free India Army, the Indian National Army.

questions.q: Bose's greeting and slogan, later adopted widely, was which phrase?
questions.explanation: Bose popularised the greeting 'Jai Hind', meaning 'Victory to India'.

questions.q: In which town was Subhas Chandra Bose born?
questions.explanation: Bose was born in 1897 at Cuttack (in present-day Odisha), into a Bengali family.

questions.q: Bose's approach to independence often put him at odds with which leader's methods?
questions.explanation: Bose frequently differed with Gandhi over the strictly nonviolent and gradualist approach.

questions.q: The all-women regiment of Bose's army was named after which heroine of 1857?
questions.explanation: The women's combat unit of the INA was called the Rani of Jhansi Regiment, after Rani Lakshmibai.

questions.q: In which year was Subhas Chandra Bose born?
questions.explanation: Bose was born on 23 January 1897 at Cuttack.

questions.q: Bose qualified for which prestigious service in 1920 but resigned to join the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Bose passed the Indian Civil Service examination in 1920 but resigned to enter the national movement.

questions.q: At which 1939 Congress session was Bose re-elected president, defeating Gandhi's preferred candidate?
questions.explanation: Bose was re-elected president at the 1939 Tripuri session, defeating Pattabhi Sitaramayya, whom Gandhi favoured.

questions.q: After resigning the Congress presidency in 1939, Bose founded which political group?
questions.explanation: Bose founded the Forward Bloc in 1939 to rally the more radical elements of the freedom movement.

questions.q: In early 1941 Bose made a dramatic secret escape from house arrest in which city?
questions.explanation: Bose escaped from British surveillance in Calcutta in January 1941 to make his way abroad.

questions.q: Bose proclaimed the Provisional Government of Free India (Azad Hind) in 1943 in which location?
questions.explanation: Bose proclaimed the Provisional Government of Azad Hind at Singapore in October 1943.

questions.q: Who had founded the Indian National Army earlier, before Bose took over its leadership in 1943?
questions.explanation: Captain Mohan Singh first organised the INA from Indian prisoners of war before Subhas Chandra Bose reorganised it.

questions.q: Which veteran revolutionary handed leadership of the Indian independence movement in East Asia to Bose?
questions.explanation: Rash Behari Bose, a senior revolutionary based in Japan, handed leadership of the movement in East Asia to Subhas Chandra Bose.

questions.q: Captain Lakshmi Sahgal led which formation of the Indian National Army under Bose?
questions.explanation: Captain Lakshmi Sahgal commanded the all-women Rani of Jhansi Regiment of the INA.

questions.q: Which Andaman and Nicobar territory did Bose's Provisional Government claim, renaming the islands?
questions.explanation: Bose renamed the Andaman and Nicobar Islands as Shaheed and Swaraj after they were placed under Azad Hind's nominal control.

questions.q: Bose first sought support for the Indian cause during the war from which European power before moving to Asia?
questions.explanation: Bose first went to Germany and sought Axis support before travelling to Southeast Asia.

questions.q: In Germany, Bose acquired which honorific title used by his Indian followers there?
questions.explanation: It was among Indians in Germany that Bose was first widely addressed as 'Netaji'.

questions.q: Bose travelled from Germany to East Asia in 1943 by which extraordinary means?
questions.explanation: Bose made a remarkable submarine journey, transferring between German and Japanese submarines, to reach East Asia.

questions.q: The INA, alongside Japanese forces, attempted to enter India through which northeastern fronts in 1944?
questions.explanation: INA and Japanese forces fought toward Imphal and Kohima in 1944 in their attempt to enter India.

questions.q: Bose's stirring book of memoirs and reflections covering his early life was titled what?
questions.explanation: Bose's autobiographical work was titled 'An Indian Pilgrim', covering his early years.

questions.q: Which song did Bose's Azad Hind movement adopt as a national anthem, a forerunner of independent India's anthem?
questions.explanation: The INA adopted 'Subh Sukh Chain', an adaptation of Tagore's verses that prefigured 'Jana Gana Mana'.

questions.q: Officers of Bose's INA were court-martialled in 1945-46 at which location, sparking nationwide protests?
questions.explanation: The INA trials were held at the Red Fort in Delhi and provoked widespread public protest.

questions.q: Three of the most prominent officers tried together at the Red Fort INA trials were Shah Nawaz Khan, Prem Sahgal and who?
questions.explanation: The famous first INA trial featured Shah Nawaz Khan, Prem Sahgal and Gurbaksh Singh Dhillon, representing three faiths.

questions.q: Bose is widely reported to have died in 1945 in a plane crash at which location, though this remains debated?
questions.explanation: Bose is reported to have died from injuries in an air crash at Taihoku (modern Taipei) on 18 August 1945, though debate persists.

questions.q: Bose had earlier authored a major political analysis of the freedom movement titled what?
options: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
questions.explanation: Bose wrote 'The Indian Struggle', a substantial account and analysis of the Indian independence movement.
